etymology.description: 大 (adult) + 口 (mouth) + 子 (child) = transmission of knowledge -> 大 corrupted to 矢 (sound component) -> 子 dropped -> 知
etymology.interpretation: The original form depicted an adult using the mouth to transmit knowledge to a child, indicating teaching or conveying awareness. Over time, this evolved into the modern form with 大 becoming 矢, acting as a phonetic component, while 子 was eventually omitted, but the core idea of exchanging knowledge was retained.

examples.chinese: 你知道这件事吗(你知道這件事嗎)？
examples.pinyin: Nǐ zhīdào zhè jiàn shì ma?
examples.english: Do you know about this matter?

examples.chinese: 他不知道该怎么做(他不知道該怎麼做)。
examples.pinyin: Tā bù zhīdào gāi zěnme zuò.
examples.english: He doesn't know what to do.

examples.chinese: 我只想知道事情的真相(我只想知道事情的真相)。
examples.pinyin: Wǒ zhǐ xiǎng zhīdào shìqíng de zhēnxiàng.
examples.english: I just want to know the truth of the matter.
